debug_info.goal:
n : ℕ
k : Fin n
S : List (Call n)
h : allKnow (after n S) k
⊢ n - 1 < S.length

failed_attempts:
-- The set of agents who know k's secret grows by at most 1 per call.
  have h_growth : ∀ S : List (Call n), (Finset.card (Finset.filter (fun j => (after S) j k) Finset.univ)) ≤ S.length + 1 := by
    intro S
    induction' S using List.reverseRecOn with S ih
    generalize_proofs at *; (
    simp +decide [ Finset.card_le_one, makeCalls, initialState ]);
    -- After adding the call ih, the set of agents who know k's secret can grow by at most 1.
    have h_growth_step : Finset.card (Finset.filter (fun j => (after (S ++ [ih])) j k) Finset.univ) ≤ Finset.card (Finset.filter (fun j => (after S) j k) Finset.univ) + 1 := by
      -- By definition of `after`, we know that `(after (S ++ [ih])) j k` holds if and only if `(after S) j k` holds or `j` is `ih.fst` or `ih.snd` and `(after S) ih.fst k` or `(after S) ih.snd k` holds.
      have h_after : ∀ j : Fin n, (after (S ++ [ih])) j k ↔ (after S) j k ∨ (j = ih.fst ∧ (after S) ih.snd k) ∨ (j = ih.snd ∧ (after S) ih.fst k) := by
        intro j
        simp [after, makeCalls, makeCall];
        grind +ring
      generalize_proofs at *; (
      by_cases h : after S ih.2 k <;> by_cases h' : after S ih.1 k <;> simp_all +decide [ Finset.filter_or, Finset.filter_eq' ] ;)
    generalize_proofs at *; (
    grind +ring);
  have := h_growth S; rw [ show Finset.filter ( fun j => after S j k ) Finset.univ = Finset.univ from Finset.filter_true_of_mem fun i _ => h i ] at this; aesop;
